what_it_does: Deterministic acceptance test for the Decision→Implementation wire (Group W / scenario S8). It injects launcher, diff_runner, and verifier implementations so the backend approve→dispatch→verify→implemented flow and its refusal paths can be exercised without burning real Claude Code sessions.
observations: Overrides Suite._self_build_commit with a no-op fake SHA to avoid real git commits during dispatches.
Uses a fresh tempfile FsStore and NodeRegistry discovery for each scenario.
Happy path asserts status=implemented, operator-only resolved=approve, and event narrative dispatch→implemented.
Implemented is not treated as a silent terminal: a new dispatcher-inert build_result_review item is surfaced for operator review.
Exactly-once dispatch is keyed on the operator resolve event's unique seq.
Bad verification, launch crashes, and scope overruns re-queue responsive review items instead of closing.
Directly exercises the governance guard with confirmed=False to prove an unverified close raises.
changed_delta is tested on a dirty tree using content-hash, not just path-set, to avoid false negatives/positives.
build_instruction is checked for declared scope and standards language (reviewed, self-description, UI/UX bar).
standouts: `implemented` status still surfaces a dispatcher-inert review item, so approving the review cannot trigger a rebuild.
Exactly-once dispatch is enforced by a decision.dispatch event keyed on the operator resolve seq.
changed_delta distinguishes pre-existing dirty files from files actually edited by the build using content hashes.
The guard backstop raises on an unverified close for code_build.
conventions: Docstring maps each test block to scenario IDs (S8.x, W1, W4, W7).
Global PASS counter with check() and raises() helpers.
Dependencies are injected via keyword arguments (launcher, verifier, runner, diff_runner).
Tests assert event kinds and payload fields, not just return values.
concerns: Relies on overriding a private method (_self_build_commit) and calling an internal guard directly, making tests brittle to internal refactors.
Uses a mutable global PASS counter rather than a test framework.
open() calls lack explicit close / context manager in the dirty-tree setup.
Temp directories are created but not explicitly cleaned up.
Some class names and consequence classes (source_data) are hardcoded without local definition.
notes: A backend acceptance harness that proves the decision-to-implementation wire behaves correctly under injected, deterministic conditions before any live operator run.
questions: What is the exact schema of events stored by FsStore and how are seq values generated?
How does changed_delta compute content hashes for files and what runner interface does it expect?
What does consequence_class source_data signify in the registry and where is the LOCKED policy declared?
What is the full lifecycle of a build_result_review item after the operator resolves it?
How does the real _self_build_commit checkpoint mechanism differ from the no-op override used here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose: it deterministically verifies the wire backend mechanisms so the live S8 run can start with confidence.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud rather than silent terminal states
intention_signals: Prove that the decision-to-implementation backend is trustworthy and safe to expose to a live operator/AI run: binds operator approval to a single dispatch, enforces review even after success, and refuses or re-queues anything unverified, out-of-scope, or locked.
novelty: The post-implementation review item is deliberately dispatcher-inert, preventing an operator 'looks good' approval from accidentally re-dispatching the same build; changed_delta uses content-hash to handle dirty trees correctly.
contribution: A deterministic, injected acceptance harness that validates the wire's bind, discriminator, exactly-once, LOCKED pre-gate, verify-or-requeue, scope-diff, and guarded-close semantics while keeping real git commits and AI sessions out of automated tests.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/wire_acceptance.py is an injected deterministic acceptance test for the Decision→Implementation wire covering happy-path dispatch, governed refusals, exactly-once, verification/launch/scope failures, guarded close, and dirty-tree delta detection.
intention_for_embedding: Provide automated, repeatable proof that the backend mechanisms of the decision-to-implementation wire hold before a live operator/AI S8 run.

symbols.does: Increments a global pass counter if a condition holds, otherwise fails with a labeled AssertionError.
symbols.description: Core assertion helper used throughout the script; on success it prints an 'ok' line and increments PASS, and on failure it raises with the supplied label.

symbols.does: Records a pass only if the supplied callable raises the expected exception type.
symbols.description: Helper that invokes fn, catches the expected exception, calls check, and fails if no exception is raised or the wrong exception type is raised.

symbols.does: Creates an isolated Suite with a temp FsStore and a no-op git commit override.
symbols.description: Factory that builds a clean test Suite, discovers nodes from the nodes directory, and replaces _self_build_commit with a lambda returning a fake 40-hex SHA so dispatches do not commit the live repository.

symbols.does: Simulates operator approval of a surfaced item and returns the resolve event's unique seq.
symbols.description: Calls suite.resolve_surfaced with 'approve', then scans the event log for the matching resolve event and returns its seq value, which is later used as the dispatch binding.

symbols.does: Returns an injected launcher that reports a successful build with the given changed files.
symbols.description: Factory for deterministic build runners that emulate a successful Claude Code session, returning a finished success result carrying the supplied changed_files list.

symbols.does: Returns an injected launcher result dict reporting a finished but unsuccessful build.
symbols.description: Deterministic build runner that returns a launch result with success=False, exit_code=1, and no changed files, used to exercise failure paths without a real session.

symbols.does: Injected runner that raises LaunchError to simulate a broken round-trip.
symbols.description: Used to prove that impl.launch fails loudly when the runner yields a non-JSON or otherwise unparseable response, rather than fabricating success.

symbols.does: Returns controlled git-style diff and untracked output for changed_delta tests.
symbols.description: Injected subprocess stand-in that returns deterministic dirty-file and untracked lists, letting the test isolate content-hash behavior from real git state.

symbols.does: Injected launcher that raises LaunchError to simulate a crashed or timed-out build session.
symbols.description: Simulates a build invocation that fails to return a result, used to verify that the wire re-queues loudly instead of silently dropping the dispatch.
